title: Viking Therapeutics Reports VK2735 Obesity Program Data at ObesityWeek 2024
text: Viking Therapeutics announced clinical trial results for VK2735, a dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist, presented at ObesityWeek 2024 in San Diego on November 4, 2024. The company presented Phase 1 data from a 28-day multiple ascending dose study of oral VK2735 tablets with key findings including the highest dose (100 mg daily) demonstrating 8.2% weight reduction from baseline, and placebo-adjusted weight loss reaching up to 6.8% across cohorts. All VK2735-treated subjects at the 100 mg dose achieved at least 5% weight loss, compared to 0% for placebo. Weight loss persisted after treatment ended, with reductions of up to 8.3% from baseline, four weeks after the last dose. The drug showed encouraging tolerability through 100 mg daily dosing with gastrointestinal adverse events predominantly mild, with 84% characterized as mild. Nausea affected 32% of treated subjects versus 11% for placebo, but no severe cases occurred. Updated data from the Phase 2 VENTURE trial (13-week weekly injections) showed weight loss up to 14.7% from baseline, with 88% of patients achieving at least 10% weight loss versus 4% for placebo. 94% of initial weight loss was maintained four weeks post-final dose. The company indicated plans to initiate Phase 2 oral studies by year-end.
